description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a predominantly white and light gray coloration, accented by streaks and patches of brown and tan. The surface is rough and uneven, with visible striations and natural textural variations that suggest sedimentary or weathered stone. One side of the rock features a more prominent brownish band, while other areas display subtle layering and mineral deposits. The rock appears solid and dense, with no visible cavities or openings. There is a faint marking or label visible on one face, possibly from a previous cataloging or study. The overall appearance suggests it could serve as a natural barrier or decorative element in an urban park or landscaped area.
description: A rough, white and gray rock with brown streaks and a rugged, layered surface, showing natural weathering and mineral patterns.
description_view_0: This view highlights the rock's broad, slanted face with prominent white and brown streaks and a rugged, uneven texture.
description_view_1: This side shows a flatter, more vertical face with subtle tan striations and a small protruding corner at the top left.
description_view_2: This angle reveals a lighter, more uniform surface with a visible label or marking and faint brownish lines.
description_view_3: This view emphasizes the rock's rough, angular edge and a pronounced brown band running diagonally across the surface.
